name: Information-seeking failures of large language models in agentic clinical reasoning
content: Information-seeking failures of large language models in agentic clinical reasoning
summary: Large language models failed in seeking information for agentic clinical reasoning tasks, highlighting their limitations in complex decision-making scenarios.
key_insights: The models struggled to effectively seek and integrate relevant medical information for clinical decisions.
This failure points to challenges in understanding context and patient-specific information within a clinical setting.
It suggests the need for further development in self-reflection and meta-cognitive abilities of language models.
xihe_relevance: 对曦和的潜在价值较低
